source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Analyses/Univariate/Bulletin17CAnalysis.cs @ c2e6192 -- A11 EXACT oracle, verified against the real RMC.BestFit Bulletin17CAnalysis by the dotnet gate. Bulletin17CAnalysis fits an LP3 model by GMM (default MultivariateNormal UQ) then computes the deterministic Cohn-style delta-method CIs (nested Gaussian quadrature over the GMM fit -- no MCMC/bootstrap seed dependence given the fitted parameters). The GMM fit uses BFGS + a numerical Jacobian (no RNG), so the parameters/point_estimates/CIs reproduce bit-identically across C++/R/Python; parameter + point_estimate match C# to rel 1e-9, the Cohn CI bounds to rel 1e-8 (the nested quadrature is a distinct summation path). confidence_level and the exceedance-probability round-trip are exact structural invariants. The 25 default exceedance ordinates ascend, so the point-estimate curve descends. X8/X9 arms: the lp3_linked_multivariate_normal and lp3_bias_corrected_bootstrap cases drive the two un-gated uncertainty methods (LinkedMultivariateNormal / BiasCorrectedBootstrap). ComputeCohnStyleConfidenceIntervals is a diagnostic delta-method computed off the RNG-free GMM point estimate ALONE (independent of UncertaintyMethod, which only governs the heavy parameter-set UQ that populates BayesianAnalysis.Results, not the Cohn curve), so those two cases assert the SAME deterministic Cohn point_estimate/CI/parameter oracle -- exercising the ParseUncertaintyMethod dispatch arms end-to-end without a chaotic seeded curve. T19: the lp3_bootstrap case drives the plain Bootstrap uncertainty method (previously untested by this file) and pins the new BootstrapDiagnostics boot_* fields. lp3_bootstrap_warm_start additionally forces clone_with_data_frame_flag TRUE (an interval-censored observation on the parent DataFrame) to exercise the Task-18 warm-start condition; ONLY the deterministic quantities are pinned there. The ensemble-aggregate mean_curve and the boot_*_retries counters are NOT pinned, and the reason is now known exactly (Task 19b; see docs/upstream-csharp-issues.md, 'Interval-censored B17C bootstrap: GMM stopping-rule knife edge'). It is NOT a BFGS port divergence: driving the REAL C# Numerics BFGS at the same near-stationary warm start on the same replicate surface reproduces the C++ stall exactly (MaximumFunctionEvaluationsReached, 2000 evaluations, ~21 evaluations per outer iteration on both sides). What differs is whether a THIRD GMM refinement pass runs at all: EstimateIterative's pass-to-pass Tools.Distance test is the only one that can stop the loop here (the companion relChange test is pinned above tolerance by the 1e-15 floor in its denominator), and the two runtimes straddle AbsoluteTolerance 1e-8 -- 1.23e-8 in C++ (a third pass runs, starts at a stationary point, stalls, and the replicate is retried) versus 3.3e-11 in C# (converged at pass 2). The residual is inherent conditioning of the censored resample fit, measured directly: perturbing a SINGLE resampled exact value on replicate idx=32 by a relative 1e-13 displaces the converged fit by 2.4e-5 (amplification ~2.4e8) and flips the third pass from stalling to succeeding, while the same perturbation at relative 1e-15 leaves the fit bit-identical; the UNRESAMPLED parent fit on the same censored frame reproduces C++-vs-C# to 3.0e-14 relative on the location parameter (3.3e-12 worst-case across the three), ruling out any upstream divergence in the GMM weighting, the moment conditions, or the T18 clone/ROS defaults. Same-language completeness is covered by the ctest test_run_bootstrap_warm_start_structural.
